name: Prediction of Neonatal and Pediatric Sepsis [Minimal]
description: Validates the federated infrastructure for pediatric data access, specifically focusing on sepsis prediction in neonatal and pediatric populations.
justification:
This task validates federated infrastructure use for **pediatric patient data access**. The initiative addresses legal complexities around protecting minors' data across EU Member States. Current data protection interpretations often prevent sharing pediatric patient information, which paradoxically hinders innovation in a field where small institutional populations make research challenging.

The focus targets **sepsis in neonatal and pediatric intensive care units**, as sepsis remains the most common cause of neonatal (less than 28 days old) and pediatric mortality. Teams will validate a recently developed early detection algorithm combining patient characteristics and monitor data to generate sepsis risk scores.

- **T6.4.1** — External validation of an early detection algorithm developed by Van Laere et al. The work assesses algorithm functionality across different neonatal populations through validation across multiple European hospital datasets.
- **T6.4.2** — Local model refinement. Teams will assess detection model performance after retraining using comprehensive datasets to improve generalizability across different populations.
- **T6.4.3** — Federated evaluation and retraining pipeline implementation. Continuous performance monitoring ensures ongoing effectiveness and patient safety following implementation.

Led by **Rob Taal** at Erasmus MC.
